name: Sales Velocity Equation
summary: Sales Velocity measures how quickly you generate revenue. The formula is: (Number of Opportunities x Average Deal Value x Win Rate) / Sales Cycle Length. Improving any one variable accelerates your revenue; improving all four transforms your performance.
whenToUseIt: Use the Sales Velocity equation during pipeline reviews, personal performance analysis, and strategic planning. It helps you diagnose exactly where your sales motion is strong and where it's leaking. If your velocity is low, the formula tells you whether to focus on generating more opportunities, increasing deal sizes, improving win rates, or shortening cycles. Sales leaders use it to compare reps, territories, and segments.
steps: Measure Number of Opportunities — Count the qualified opportunities entering your pipeline in a given period. More opportunities means more at-bats. Improve this by: prospecting more effectively, improving lead quality from marketing, expanding into new segments, or getting more referrals from existing customers.

Measure Average Deal Value — Calculate the average contract value (ACV) of your closed deals. Higher deal values mean more revenue per win. Improve this by: selling to larger companies, bundling products, focusing on ROI to justify premium pricing, or upselling additional features during the sales cycle.

Measure Win Rate — What percentage of opportunities convert to closed-won? Higher win rates mean you're converting more of your pipeline. Improve this by: better qualification (disqualify bad fits earlier), stronger discovery, better demos, more effective objection handling, or developing stronger champions.

Measure Sales Cycle Length — How many days from opportunity creation to close? Shorter cycles mean faster revenue. Improve this by: creating urgency with compelling events, getting all stakeholders involved early, providing ROI calculators and business cases that speed internal approval, and proactively managing the buyer's process.

Calculate and Optimise — Sales Velocity = (Opportunities x Deal Value x Win Rate) / Cycle Length. Run the numbers monthly. Even small improvements in each variable compound dramatically. A 10% improvement in all four variables increases velocity by 46%.
example:
Two AEs at the same SaaS company, same territory:

AE 1 (Alex): 30 opportunities x $40K ACV x 25% win rate / 90 days = $3,333/day velocity
AE 2 (Sam): 20 opportunities x $55K ACV x 35% win rate / 60 days = $6,417/day velocity

Sam generates nearly double the revenue velocity despite having fewer opportunities. Why? Higher average deal values (better targeting and value selling), better win rate (stronger discovery and qualification), and shorter cycles (better process management and urgency creation).

For Alex, the diagnosis is clear: win rate (25%) and cycle length (90 days) are the biggest drags. Coaching should focus on qualification (stop chasing bad deals) and process management (create urgency, multi-thread, get all stakeholders involved early).
